title: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel
display_title: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel
wikitext:
[]: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel?             Quote Post by AsukaShikinami10 » Tue Jul 02, 2024 1:47 am So lately I've been thinking about the missing 11th angel since it isn't even mentioned in NTE. Someone here put out there the idea that the Nebuchadnezzar Key might be the 11th angel and after rewatching the first minutes of 2.22 I came to realize something that may be key (no pun intended) into this. When Kaji is giving Gendo the briefcase containing said key, he mentions something about it being the LOST number. It might be a hint that the 11th angel was already caught and kept in containment as the 3rd angel was. Maybe NERV went one step ahead with the help of the Dead Sea Scrolls and found the 11th angel in permafrost, so they took advantage of that and reduced it to some kind of nervous system attached to a syringe. The 3rd angel may have been found somewhere else and ended up contained in the Taghba base along the Nebuchadnezar Key. Let's not forget Gendo obtained angel-like powers once he injected the Nebuchadnezar Key into his own body.

[]: Re: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Thu Aug 01, 2024 10:19 pm >When Gendo fuses with the Key he becomes a god >Attaining godhood in Evangelion is something that happens when Adam and Lilith based life interact >The Key was under HEAVY security, Kaji had to use the 3rd Angel as a distraction to get it out of the base shit it lines up, definitely plausible

[]: Re: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel?             Quote Post by MikeRobert » Sat Aug 03, 2024 5:17 am That's a fascinating theory! The "lost number" detail is intriguing, and it makes sense that NERV would try to get ahead of the Angels. The idea of the 11th Angel being reduced to a neural system is a wild card that could explain a lot. Definitely food for thought!

[]: Re: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel?             Quote Post by AsukaShikinami10 » Fri Aug 23, 2024 9:55 pm Watching again 3.333 and 3.0+1.11 I noticed something interesting that may help contribute with this theory. We see the Failures of Infinity (in their Eva-01 form and their female body form later) all lacking a head. It is clearly stated that they are the next step for human evolution by changing the fruit of knowledge for the fruit of life in order to prevail. Were else have we seen headless beings? Marks 07, 09, 10, 11 & 12 all lack a proper head (As the latter 4 seem to have an improvised one while Marks.07 have a mechanical skull attached to them), and they are all Adam-based life forms. So, following this pattern, we can say all beings blessed with the fruit of life lack a proper neuronal system (As in head). That's were we can get the clues as to why the Nebuchadnezar Key might be the missing 11th angel. It is a nervous system lacking a head as all the mentioned adams/angel life forms mentioned below. The original plan of getting rid of all the angels, even when Gendo absorbed the Nebuchadnezar Key (thus becoming an angelic being), was a task completed and let me explain why. After the 9th angel inside Asuka was killed by Evangelion 13, Gendo literally put himself inside the eva's mouth letting it eat him alive, finally getting the last remaining angel killed/absorbed. Now concluding his plan he procceded to take control of the Evangelion 13 through said absorsion.

[]: Re: Is the Nebuchadnezar Key the 11th angel?             Quote Post by Cola-09 » Fri Sep 20, 2024 12:34 pm Interesting take. However, I wonder how it works with Kaworu's demotion? As far as I remember the narrative was that he becomes the 13. because Gendo made himself an angel? If the key really was an angel how would the numbers work?
